prompt:
Write a FIELD Brief for NBA Finals Game 4. FIELD voice: hard news lead, rhetorical flourish, no wire-copy grammar. Three-part arc required: opening scene + turning point + consequence. 120-160 words. No bullet points.

CONFIRMED HISTORICAL RECORD: The Knicks erased a 27-point halftime deficit — the largest halftime comeback in NBA playoff history. This MUST be in the lead sentence, stated as a confirmed record.

FINAL: NYK 107, SAS 106
NBA FINALS SERIES: NYK leads 3-1

LINESCORE:
Q1: SAS 41 - NYK 22 (SAS +19)
Q2: SAS 35 - NYK 27 (Halftime: SAS 76-49, SAS up 27)
Q3: SAS 14 - NYK 26 (End Q3: SAS 90-75, SAS up 15)
Q4: SAS 16 - NYK 32 (Final: NYK 107-106)

SECOND HALF: NYK outscored SAS 58-30

GAME LEADERS:
NYK: OG Anunoby - 33 pts, 4 reb, 1 ast
SAS: Devin Vassell - 18 pts, 5 reb, 4 ast

SERIES CONTEXT: SAS stole Game 3 on the road. SAS led by 27 at halftime. NYK outscored SAS 32-16 in Q4. SAS faces elimination in Game 5 in San Antonio. NYK one win from the championship.

LEAD REQUIREMENT: The first sentence must contain the phrase "largest halftime deficit in NBA playoff history" or equivalent. Do not hedge this — it is a confirmed record.
